senses.gloss: the Mawlid; the birth of Prophet Muhammad, or the celebration that accompanies that day
a day marking the birth of an important historical Islamic figure (esp. Ahl al-Bayt), or the celebration thereof.

examples.text: شَهْرُ شَعْبَانَ مُهِمٌّ عِنْدَ الشِّيعَةِ لِكِثْرَةِ الْمَوَالِدِ فِيهِ.
examples.english: The month of Sha'ban is important for the Shia due to the numerous mawlids in it.

senses.gloss: Arabized; raised or brought up under Arabs
Arabized; culturally Arabized while being racially non-Arab
half-bred, mongrel; racially impure, of mixed parentage or lineage, being a half-breed

examples.text:
رَأَيْتُكَ تُوسِعُ ٱلشُّعَرَاءَ نَيْلًا / حَدِيثَهُمُ ٱلْمُوَلَّدَ وَٱلْقَدِيمَا
فَتُعْطِي مَنْ بَقَى مَالًا جَسِيمًا / وَتُعْطِي مَنْ مَضَى شَرَفًا عَظِيمَا
سَمِعْتُكَ مُنْشِدًا بَيْتَيْ زِيَادٍ / نَشِيدًا مِثْلَ مُنْشِدِهِ كَرِيمَا
فَمَا أَنْكَرْتُ مَوْضِعَهُ وَلَٰكِنْ / غَبَطْتُ بِذَاكَ أَعْظُمَهُ ٱلرَّمِيمَا

etymology.text:
Noun of place respectively verbal noun from the root و ل د (w l d). Compare Hebrew מוֹלֶדֶת (molédet, mōléḏeṯ).

Derived from the active participle from وَلَّدَ (wallada).

Derived from the passive participle from وَلَّدَ (wallada).

body.text: (T, S, M, A, Msb) of a man. (S, L, &c.) ―         -b2-  See also
